action.text: After the disciple casts one of its limited-use spells or makes a Glaive or Elemental Strike attack on its turn, it makes one Elemental Strike attack. This attack deals 8 (2d4 + 3) acid, bludgeoning, or thunder damage (disciple's choice) for a melee attack or 5 (2d4) damage for a ranged attack.
action.attack: Melee Acid, Bludgeoning, Or Thunder Damage||2d4+3
Ranged Acid, Bludgeoning, Or Thunder Damage||2d4

monster.spells: Concussion (TP), Dash Strike (HB), Earthen Fist (HB), Misty Step, Blur, Earthen Uppercut (HB), Entangle, Longstrider, Magic Missile
monster.description:
The disciple of ferocious dust is a Disciple of Ferocity bender that has affinity with air and earth. It combines the long reach of its glaive with movement-enhancing spells such as longstrider and misty step to conduct devastating hit-and-run tactics. If ambushing a target, the disciple begins combat with longstrider in effect and casts entangle to immobilize foes. On a subsequent turn, it uses either earthen fist or earthen uppercut to knock restrained foes prone. Using its Pole Vaulter trait, it leaps over this difficult terrain to move in for the kill. In retreat, the disciple uses blur and misty step, living to fight another day.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 359 (Indie)

monster.name: Avatar of Invigorating Elements

trait.name: Blessed Aura
trait.text: Each creature of the avatar's choice within 30 feet of the avatar is under the effects of the bless spell.

trait.name: Distracting Onslaught (5/Day) (1/Turn)
trait.text: When the avatar deals acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage to a creature, it can create a distracting array. The next time that creature makes an attack roll or saving throw before the end of its next turn, the creature must roll a d8 and subtract the value from the result.

trait.name: Freedom of Movement
trait.text: The avatar is always under the effects of the freedom of movement spell.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
trait.text: If the avatar fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Primordial Avatar State (1/Day)
trait.text: If the avatar would be reduced to 0 hit points, its current hit point total instead resets to 150, it gains 40 temporary hit points, its AC increases to 19, its attacks deal an extra 7 (2d6) fire or lightning damage on a hit (avatar's choice when it makes the attack), and it gains a flying, walking, and swimming speed of 60 feet. Additionally, the avatar can now use the options in the "Avatar Actions" section for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 11,500 XP (23,000 XP total) for defeating the avatar after its Primordial Avatar State activates.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The avatar is a 9th-level spellcaster. It uses Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 18, +10 to hit with spell attacks). The avatar has the following bender spells prepared:
• At will: calm air*(c) , calm earth*(c) , calm flames*(c) , calm waters*(c) • 3/Day each: haste(c)(p), flaming tiger leap*, flashbang*, misty step, shield • 2/Day each: cone of cold, cyclone*(c) , steelskin*(p) , Raiko's rending rage*
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The avatar has two other spells of 1st level or higher from any bender spell list prepared (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The avatar makes four attacks with any combination of Unarmed Strike and Elemental Strike.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Elemental Strike
action.text: Melee or Ranged Spell Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d6 + 5) acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (avatar's choice) for a melee attack, or 7 (1d4 + 5) damage for a ranged attack.
action.attack: Melee Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Or Thunder Damage|+10|1d6+5
Ranged Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Or Thunder Damage|+10|1d4+5

action.name: Elemental Combo (Bonus Action)
action.text: After the avatar casts one of its limited-use spells or makes an unarmed strike or Elemental Strike attack on its turn, it makes one Elemental Strike attack. This attack deals 15 (4d4 + 5) acid, bludgeoning, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder damage (avatar's choice) for a melee attack or 10 (4d4) damage for a ranged attack.
action.attack: Melee Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Or Thunder Damage||4d4+5
Ranged Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Or Thunder Damage||4d4

reaction.name: Primordial Pulse
reaction.text: Trigger: A creature the avatar can see within 30 feet of it misses an attack roll or fails a saving throw. Response: The avatar grants the creature a rejuvenating boost. The creature immediately rerolls the attack roll or saving throw, with a +5 bonus.

legendary.name: Legendary Actions
legendary.text: The avatar can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. The avatar regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn

legendary.name: Leaping Strike
legendary.text: The avatar leaps up to 25 feet without provoking opportunity attacks and can then make one Unarmed Strike or Elemental Strike attack.

legendary.name: Invigorate (5/Day)
legendary.text: The avatar restores 15 hit points to a creature it can see within 30 feet of it.

legendary.name: Avatar Actions
legendary.text: If the avatar's Primordial Avatar State trait has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as legendary actions.

legendary.name: Cast a Spell (Avatar Actions)
legendary.text: The avatar casts one of the following spells: bloodweave*, mireball*, wind strike*, or wanyūdō's fury*.

legendary.name: Mass Invigorate (5/Day) (Avatar Actions)
legendary.text: The avatar restores 15 hit points to up to three different creatures it can see within 30 feet of it.

monster.spells: Calm Air (HB), Calm Earth (HB), Calm Flames (HB), Calm Waters (HB), Haste, Flaming Tiger Leap (HB), Flashbang (HB), Misty Step, Shield, Cone of Cold, Cyclone (HB), Steelskin (HB), Raiko's Rending Rage (HB), Bloodweave (HB), Mireball (TP), Wind Strike (HB), Or Wanyūdō's Fury (HB)
monster.description:
The avatar of invigorating elements is a Disciple of Invigoration bender that has affinity with all four elements. It typically fights with other creatures that benefit from its Blessed Aura trait, Primordial Pulse reaction, and Invigorate legendary action. If ambushing, the avatar will typically pre-cast haste and steelskin on itself and begin combat with flashbang if it has nonlethal intent, or cone of cold if it aims to kill. After an enemy takes its turn, the avatar performs a Leaping Strike legendary action against that creature so that it can trigger its Distracting Onslaught trait and impose a d8 penalty to one of the enemy's rolls. On its next turn, the avatar chooses an area spell to affect as many affected enemies as possible. If it can't target more than one creature with its area of effect, it resorts to Elemental Strikes, using misty step to better locate itself for the next round of combat.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 360 (Indie)

monster.name: Shrine Warden Curator

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The shrine warden is an 8th-level spellcaster. It uses Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks). The shrine warden has the following cleric spells prepared:
• At will: guidance(c) , light, sacred flame • 3/day each: bless(c)(p), cure wounds, guiding bolt, silence(c) , spiritual weapon • 1/day each: extract shirikodama*, glyph of warding(p) , guardian of faith
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration, p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The shrine warden has three other cleric spells of 1st level or higher prepared (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Warhammer
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d8 + 2) bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) radiant damage.

action.name: Sacred Flame
action.text: Saving Throw: DC 14 Dexterity, range 60 ft., one creature the shrine warden can see. Failure: 9 (2d8) radiant damage. The target gains no benefit from cover for this saving throw.

action.name: Consecration (1 per short or long rest) (Bonus Action)
action.text:
The shrine warden conjures a shrine at a point on the ground that it can see within 60 feet of itself that radiates magic in a 20-foot radius. It chooses up to four creatures it can see to be affected by one of the following effects:
Blessing of Power. When an affected creature in the aura hits a target with an attack roll, it can deal an extra 3 (1d6) radiant damage to that target. It then can't gain this benefit again until the start of its next turn.
Curse of Panic. An affected creature that attempts to enter the aura for the first time on its turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of the shrine until the start of its next turn.

reaction.name: Shrine Guardian (4/Day)
reaction.text: Trigger: A creature in the shrine warden's shrine's aura makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Response: The shrine warden rolls a d4 and applies it as a bonus or penalty (shrine warden's choice) to the creature's roll.

monster.spells: Guidance, Light, Sacred Flame, Bless, Cure Wounds, Guiding Bolt, Silence, Spiritual Weapon, Extract Shirikodama (HB), Glyph of Warding, Guardian of Faith
monster.description:
The shrine warden curator is a cleric that specializes in supporting its team and preparing traps for foes. The shrine warden casts glyph of warding (typically choosing Explosive Runes with thunder damage) in preparation for an ambush, and casts bless on itself and its allies moments before combat begins. It likes to begin combat by summoning a guardian of faith and using its Consecration bonus action to summon a Curse of Panic shrine. From this safe haven, it creates a spiritual weapon and casts guiding bolt and extract shirikodama. If fighting spellcasters, it carefully positions its silence spell to force them to choose between taking damage from the guardian of faith, or being unable to cast spells with verbal components.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 362 (Indie)

monster.name: Yokai Druid Kincaller

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The druid is a 12th-level spellcaster. It uses Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 16, +8 to hit with spell attacks). The druid has the following druid spells prepared:
• At will: concussion*, druidcraft, shillelagh, smokescreen*(c) • 3/day each: cure wounds, depth charge*, earthskin*(p), lifesap aura*(p), longstrider(p) • 1/day each: dominate person(c), lion's roar*(c), eruption*, Nomi's adamantine carapace*(c)
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The druid has eight other druid spells of 1st level or higher prepared (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Quarterstaff
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) bludgeoning damage, or 5 (1d8 +1) bludgeoning damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Shillelagh
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) bludgeoning damage. The druid can only make this attack if it has cast shillelagh on its quarterstaff.

action.name: Concussion
action.text: Saving Throw: DC 16 Dexterity, range 60 ft., one creature the druid can see. Failure: 13 (3d8) thunder damage, and the target is deafened until the start of the druid's next turn.

reaction.name: Yokai Wild Shape (2 times)
reaction.text: Trigger: The druid casts a spell with a casting time of one action. Response: The druid transforms into a Beast, Celestial, Construct, Fey, Fiend, or Plant that has the Yokai tag and a CR no higher than 2 for up to 6 hours (such as a stone komainu or kappa, see table on page 177). While in a new form, the druid's stat block is replaced by the stat block of that form, except the druid keeps its Spells section, languages, and ability to speak. While in this form, it has advantage on Constitution saving throws, and spells it casts don't require somatic or material components. The druid reverts to its true form if it falls unconscious, drops to 0 hit points, or dies. The druid can revert to its true form using a bonus action.

monster.spells: Concussion (TP), Druidcraft, Shillelagh, Smokescreen (TP), Cure Wounds, Depth Charge (TP), Earthskin (HB), Lifesap Aura (HB), Longstrider, Dominate Person, Lion's Roar (HB), Eruption (Ryoko) (HB), Nomi's Adamantine Carapace (HB)
monster.description:
This druid is a frontline specialist. When ambushing, it imbues itself with non-concentration spells, such as longstrider, lifesap aura, and earthskin, before barreling into the enemy, often in the Wild Shape form of a protective stone komainu or grappling kappa. The spell it casts on its first turn in combat depends on its intention; if it aims to soak foes' attacks, it casts Nomi's adamantine carapace. If it has lethal intent, it casts lion's roar. If its aim is to control the opposition, it casts dominate person.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 363 (Indie)

monster.name: Skeletal Warblade

trait.name: One with Nothing (1/Day)
trait.text: When the warblade is reduced to 0 hit points, it instead is reduced to 1 hit point and immediately teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see, leaving behind a plume of mist.

trait.name: Skeletal Form
trait.text: The warblade's reach is 10 feet, its melee attacks deal an extra 4 (1d8) necrotic damage (included in the attacks), and it gains 5 temporary hit points at the start of each of its turns. In addition, when a creature starts its turn grappled by the warblade, the creature takes 13 (2d12) magical piercing damage. The warblade can't be disarmed of its Osseous Spike.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The warblade makes three Osseous Spike or Javelin attacks. It can use Skewer in place of two of these attacks.

action.name: Osseous Spike
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) magical piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage.

action.name: Javelin
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage, plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage for a melee attack.

action.name: Skewer (3/Day)
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) magical piercing damage plus 4 (1d8) necrotic damage, and the target is grappled by the warblade (escape DC 15). This grapple ends early if the warblade uses the weapon to make another attack. When the grapple ends, the target takes 13 (3d8) piercing damage.

action.name: Blitzer (Bonus Action)
action.text: The warblade attempts to shove or grapple a creature within its reach

action.name: Disengage (Bonus Action)
action.text: The warblade takes the Disengage action.

monster.description:
The skeletal warblade is a fighter that specializes in locking down and isolating targets before finishing them off. It starts combat by flying over any melee enemies and using Skewer to grapple the enemy with the lowest Strength score, using its Blitzer bonus action to try again if Skewer misses. The next round, it Disengages (as a bonus action) and Dashes upwards, using the grappled creature to provide half-cover against ranged attacks originating from below it.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 364 (Indie)

monster.name: Novice of the Fifth Gate

trait.name: Distant Striker
trait.text: The normal and long ranges of attacks the novice makes with Thrown weapons is doubled (included in the attacks), and if it misses with a Thrown weapon attack while hidden, its position is not revealed.

trait.name: Nimble Duelist
trait.text: Creatures the novice hits with a melee attack using its Meteor Hammer (or any other Finesse weapon) can't make opportunity attacks against it until the end of the turn.

trait.name: Unarmored Defense
trait.text: While the novice is wearing no armor and wielding no shield, its AC includes its Wisdom modifier.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The novice makes two attacks with any combination of Unarmed Strike, Meteor Hammer, and Shuriken. It can use Whirling Strike in place of one attack.

action.name: Meteor Hammer
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Unarmed Strike
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) magical bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Shuriken
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, range 40/120 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d6 + 2) piercing damage.

action.name: Whirling Strike (2/Day)
action.text: Each creature within 10 feet of the novice must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success. Success or failure, a creature can't make opportunity attacks against the novice until the end of the turn.

action.name: Ki (8/Short or Long Rest) (Bonus Action)
action.text:
The novice performs one of the following:
Flurry of Blows (After Multiattack action). The novice makes two Unarmed Strike attacks.
Patient Defense. The novice takes the Dodge action and gains 2 temporary hit points.
Step of the Wind. The novice takes the Dash and Disengage actions and gains 2 temporary hit points. Until the end of its turn, its jump distance is doubled.

reaction.name: Deflect Missiles
reaction.text: Trigger: The novice takes damage from a ranged weapon attack. Response: The novice reduces the damage taken by 13 (1d10 + 8). If the damage is reduced to 0, it can expend one use of Ki (see Bonus Actions) to make a ranged attack (range 20/60 ft.) with the weapon or piece of ammunition it deflected as part of this reaction (+4 to hit, 5 (1d6 + 2) damage on a hit).

reaction.name: Slow Fall
reaction.text: Trigger: The novice takes falling damage. Response: The novice reduces the damage taken by 30.

monster.description:
The novice of the fifth gate is a monk that follows the Way of Eight Gates. To stealthily pick off targets, it uses its shuriken and Distant Striker trait to remain unseen. In a blitzing ambush, it uses its high speed and Step of the Wind to find a place amongst many enemies where it can use its Whirling Strike. In combat, it uses Patient Defense when it has no temporary hit points, and otherwise uses Flurry of Blows. If acting as an assassin, it may use only its Flurry of Blows, fleeing as soon as its task is accomplished. When fleeing, it makes use of Step of the Wind.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 365 (Indie)

monster.name: Yojimbo Knight
monster.save: Str +7, Dex +3, Con +6, Int +3, Wis +7, Cha +9

trait.name: Aura of Protection
trait.text: Whenever the knight or a creature of its choice within 10 feet of it makes a saving throw, the creature gains a +3 bonus to the saving throw. The knight must be conscious to grant this bonus.

trait.name: Direct Damage
trait.text: The knight's Warhammer attacks bypass temporary hit points, and, if it hits a creature that is concentrating on a spell with a Warhammer attack, that creature has disadvantage on the saving throw made to maintain its concentration.

trait.name: Inner Divinity
trait.text: Whenever the knight hits a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 4 (1d8) radiant damage (included in the attacks).

trait.name: Smite (3/Day)
trait.text: When the knight hits a creature with a melee attack, it can deal an extra 9 (2d8) radiant damage to the creature.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The knight is a 6th-level spellcaster. It uses Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks). The knight has the following paladin spells prepared:
• 2/Day each: cure wounds, shield of faith(c), shielding word* • 1/Day each: earthskin*(p), lifesap aura*(p), spirit guardians(c), switcheroo*
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration, p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The knight has two other paladin spells of 1st level or higher prepared (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The knight makes two Warhammer or Javelin attacks. It can use Shockwave in place of one of these attacks.

action.name: Warhammer
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) radiant damage, and the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn (this effect can stack).

action.name: Javelin
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage, plus 4 (1d8) radiant damage for a melee attack.

action.name: Shockwave (3/Day)
action.text: Each creature within 10 feet of the knight must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or take 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage and be pushed 10 feet directly away from the knight.

action.name: Lay on Hands (60 Charges/Day)
action.text: The knight touches a non-Undead, non-Construct creature within its reach and spends any number of charges to restore an equal number of hit points to the creature. Alternatively, it can expend 5 charges to cure the target of one disease or neutralise one poison affecting it. It can cure multiple diseases and neutralise multiple poisons with a single use of Lay on Hands, expending charges separately for each one.

reaction.name: Daring Protector
reaction.text: Trigger: A creature makes a melee attack against another creature within 10 feet of the knight. Response: The knight moves up to its speed towards the attacker. If it ends its movement within 5 feet of the attacker, the knight becomes the target of the attack instead.

reaction.name: Guardian's Intervention (1 per short or long rest)
reaction.text: Trigger: A creature the knight can see within 30 feet of it takes damage from an attack. Response: The knight reduces the damage taken by 33 (6d10). If this reduces the damage to 0, the creature can use its reaction to make one weapon attack against the creature that attacked it.

monster.spells: Cure Wounds, Shield of Faith, Shielding Word (TP), Earthskin (HB), Lifesap Aura (HB), Spirit Guardians, Switcheroo
monster.description:
The yojimbo knight is a juggernaut of a paladin sworn to the Oath of Yojimbo. In combat, its role is to position itself within 10 feet of its most vulnerable allies, giving them the benefit of its Aura of Protection and allowing the knight to use its Daring Protector reaction to become the target of melee attacks. Its allies know this and stick close. For those beyond the range of this reaction, it uses the shielding word spell. It restores 50 hit points with its first use of Lay on Hands, saving its final 10 charges to revive a downed ally. When it ambushes a target, the knight casts lifesap aura and earthskin just before springing its trap. Offensively, it casts spirit guardians and positions itself in the midst of its enemies to take advantage of its Shockwave action, weathering any opportunity attacks this repositioning may trigger. If enemies are too scattered for the aforementioned tactics, it targets spellcasters with its attacks and Smite trait. The yojimbo knight is often the last to flee, letting its allies make good their escape.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 366 (Indie)

monster.name: Rōnin Scarred

trait.name: Land's Stride
trait.text: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs the rōnin no extra movement, and it can pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them or taking damage from them.

trait.name: Masterless
trait.text: The rōnin has advantage on saving throws made to avoid the charmed condition.

trait.name: Slash Draw
trait.text: Twice per turn, the rōnin can draw or stow a weapon, no action required. The first time each turn that it hits a creature with a weapon it drew that turn, it deals an extra 3 (1d6) damage. The rōnin has at least two wakizashis.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The rōnin is a 4th-level spellcaster. It uses Wisdom as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks). The rōnin knows the following ranger spells:
• 2/Day each: cure wounds, hunter's mark(c), longstrider(p) • 1/Day each: earthskin*(p), expeditious retreat(c), snakebite*(c)
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The rōnin knows two other ranger spells of 1st level or higher (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The rōnin makes three Wakizashi attacks or two Longbow attacks.

action.name: Wakizashi
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) piercing damage. See Slash Draw.

action.name: Longbow
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d10 + 4) piercing damage.

action.name: Hamstring (3/Day)
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 10 (1d6 + 7) slashing damage and, until the end of the creature's next turn, its AC is reduced by a value equal to its Dexterity modifier (minimum 0), and its speed is reduced by 15 feet.

reaction.name: Cleaving Parry
reaction.text: Trigger: The ronin is hit by a melee attack while wielding a melee weapon. Response: The rōnin gains a +3 bonus to its AC against the triggering attack, potentially causing it to miss.

monster.spells: Cure Wounds, Hunter's Mark, Longstrider, Earthskin (HB), Expeditious Retreat, Snakebite (HB)
monster.description:
The rōnin scarred is a solitary ranger skilled in one-on-one dueling. It typically has longstrider cast and, when ambushing, casts earthskin and snakebite before beginning combat. In the first round of a fight, the rōnin uses Hamstring, then focuses all its attacks on the hamstrung creature. To flee, it casts expeditious retreat, using the Disengage action and Dash as a bonus action.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 368 (Indie)

monster.name: Tamaya Journeyman
monster.skill: Acrobatics +7, Deception +3, Investigation +5, Sleight of Hand +7, Stealth +7

trait.name: Evasion
trait.text: When the journeyman is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Sneak Attack (1/Turn)
trait.text: The journeyman deals an extra 14 (4d6) damage when it hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally of the journeyman that isn't incapacitated and the journeyman doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

trait.name: Tinkered Artillery (2/Short or Long Rest)
trait.text:
Before the journeyman makes a Rifle attack, it can choose to use a piece of special ammunition, choosing from the options below.
Poisonous. On a hit, the target takes an extra 3 (1d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of the journeyman's next turn.
Whomping. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC 13 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Rapier
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Rifle
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 120/480 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d12 + 3) piercing damage. Reload (2): the journeyman must use an action or bonus action to reload this weapon after it has been fired twice. Loud (500).

action.name: Cunning Action (Bonus Action)
action.text: The journeyman takes the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

action.name: Spellbomb (Bonus Action)
action.text:
The journeyman throws a spellbomb at a point within 60 feet of it, choosing a bomb from the following options, which have the effects of spells but do not require any components and last their full duration (no concentration required). The bomb doesn't detonate on landing, but requires the journeyman to take the Detonate reaction, creating the spell's effect centered on the bomb.
• 2/Day each: entangle, fog cloud • 1/Day each: flashbang*, shatter
*See Chapter 13 - Spells

action.name: Steady Aim (Bonus Action)
action.text: The journeyman gains advantage on its next attack roll this turn. It can't use this bonus action if it has moved this turn, and after it uses it, its speed is reduced to 0 feet until the end of the turn.

reaction.name: Detonate
reaction.text: Trigger: A creature the journeyman can see ends its turn. Response: The journeyman detonates one of its spellbombs that is within 90 feet of it.

monster.description:
A master of ambush, the tamaya journeyman is a rogue specialising in ranged attacks and debilitating spellbombs. In an ideal ambush, the journeyman gives spellbombs to its allies, and it positions itself close enough (within 90 feet) to the fray so that it can detonate the bombs whilst using its own bonus action to steady its aim. If a foe is adjacent to the journeyman's allies, the journeyman will use its Whomping ammunition to knock the target prone, giving its allies advantage on their attack rolls. Otherwise, it uses Poisonous ammunition. To flee or reposition, the journeyman takes the Disengage action, throws an entangle spellbomb, and then runs away, detonating the spellbomb once out of its area of effect.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 369 (Indie)

monster.name: Caller of Destruction

trait.name: Durable Connection
trait.text: The caller has advantage on saving throws made to maintain its concentration on its manifested spirit.

trait.name: Metamagic
trait.text:
The caller can change the spells it casts in the following ways:
Quickened Spell (3/Day). When the caller casts a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, it can change the casting time to 1 bonus action for that casting.
Twinned Spell (1/Day). When the caller casts a spell that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, it can target a second creature in range with the same spell.
Subtle Spell (3/Day). When the caller casts a spell, it can cast it without any verbal or somatic components.

trait.name: Spirit Synchronicity
trait.text: Spells the caller casts that don't have a range of self can originate from the caller's manifested spirit, instead of the caller, if it is within 120 feet of the caller.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The caller is a 14th-level spellcaster. It uses Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 17, +9 to hit with spell attacks). The caller knows the following sorcerer spells:
• At will: earthen fist*, fire bolt, message, pins & needles*, smokescreen*(c) , soften descent* • 4/Day each: counterspell, depth charge*, mage armor(p) , misty step • 2/Day each: cone of cold, endure*, guardian of faith • 1/Day each: disintegrate, teleport
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The caller knows four other sorcerer spells of 1st level or higher (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Dagger
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d4 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Earthen Fist
action.text: Melee Spell Attack: +9 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: 13 (3d8) bludgeoning damage, and the target must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failure, the caller can either knock the creature prone or push it up to 15 feet directly away from the caller.

action.name: Fire Bolt
action.text: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 16 (3d10) fire damage.

action.name: Pins & Needles
action.text: Saving Throw: DC 17 Constitution, range 60 ft., one creature the caller can see. Failure: 13 (3d8) psychic damage and, if the target failed its save by 5 or more, it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

action.name: Manifest Spirit (2/Short or Long Rest)
action.text: The caller conjures a spirit of destruction within 30 feet of it that remains for 10 minutes, or until the caller loses its concentration (as if concentrating on a spell). The spirit shares the caller's initiative count but takes its turn immediately after the caller. The caller can command the spirit as an action or bonus action; otherwise, the spirit takes the Dodge or Help action.

monster.spells: Earthen Fist (HB), Fire Bolt, Message, Pins & Needles (TP), Smokescreen (TP), Soften Descent (HB), Counterspell, Depth Charge (TP), Mage Armor, Misty Step, Cone of Cold, Endure (TP), Guardian of Faith, Disintegrate, Teleport
monster.description:
A caller of destruction is a sorcerer who manifests its presence into a wraith-like spirit entity. The caller of destruction moulds this entity into a swift, aggressive creature that explodes upon death, decimating those around it. When preparing for an ambush, the caller positions a guardian of faith to either block an escape or provide a barrier behind which it can hide (if its quarry is close, it uses its Subtle Spell Metamagic option to escape notice). It uses its Manifest Spirit action, commanding the spirit to hide in a shadowy, advantageous position. The caller's first spell in combat is disintegrate, which it twins using its Twinned Spell Metamagic option. It follows this up with a Quickened Spell cone of cold or depth charge, using its action to cast a cantrip. To flee, the caller casts teleport.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 370 (Indie)

monster.name: Spirit of Destruction
monster.languages: understands the languages its caller speaks

trait.name: Detonator
trait.text: When the spirit is reduced to 0 hit points, each creature of the caller's choice within 20 feet of the spirit must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or take 25 (2d10 + 14) necrotic damage.

trait.name: Frenzy
trait.text: The spirit has advantage on all melee attack rolls, and all attack rolls against it have advantage.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The spirit makes two attacks with any combination of Spirit Grasp and Spirit Rend.

action.name: Spirit Grasp
action.text: Melee Spell Attack: +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) necrotic or radiant damage (caller's choice).

action.name: Spirit Rend
action.text: Ranged Spell Attack: +9 to hit, range 60 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) necrotic or radiant damage (caller's choice)

action.name: Detect (Bonus Action)
action.text: The spirit makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

action.name: Teleport (Bonus Action)
action.text: The spirit teleports up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

monster.description: Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 371 (Indie)

monster.name: Shinigami Shadow String

trait.name: Talisman (2/Day)
trait.text: When the shadow string fails an ability check, it can add a d4 to the roll, potentially turning the roll into a success.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The shadow string is a 4th-level spellcaster. It uses Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks). The shadow string knows the following warlock spells:
• At will: eldritch blast, mage armor(p), pins & needles* • 2/Day each: hellish rebuke, ice moon* • 1/Day each: mirror image, misty step
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The shadow string knows one other warlock spell of 1st level or higher (GM's choice). It can cast that spell in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Dagger
action.text: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) piercing damage.

action.name: Eldritch Blast
action.text: Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 120 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) force damage.

action.name: Pins & Needles
action.text: Saving Throw: DC 12 Constitution, range 60 ft., one creature the shadow string can see. Failure: 4 (1d8) psychic damage and, if the target failed the save by 5 or more, it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

action.name: Grim Puppeteer (3/Day)
action.text:
The shadow string takes control of a Large or smaller corpse or unconscious creature it can see within 60 feet of it with a CR or character level of 4 or lower, which gains 22 temporary hit points. For the next minute, or until the puppet loses its temporary hit points, the puppet shares the shadow string's initiative count and takes its turn immediately after the shadow string. The shadow string can use a bonus action on its turn to command the puppet to take an action, which can be one in its stat block (or in the stat block it had in life if it's a corpse) or some other action. The puppet can't use any reactions, legendary actions, lair actions, or any actions that create or summon additional creatures. In addition, the puppet:
• Is treated as an Undead if it's a corpse. • Can't regain hit points or recover from the unconscious condition. • Is immune to the charmed and frightened conditions. • Uses the shadow string's spell attack modifier for its attack rolls (+4) and spellcasting ability modifier as a bonus to its damage rolls (+2).
When this effect ends, the shadow string can't use this action on that corpse or creature again until the shadow string finishes a long rest.

monster.spells: Eldritch Blast, Mage Armor, Pins & Needles (TP), Hellish Rebuke, Ice Moon (HB), Mirror Image, Misty Step
monster.description:
The shinigami shadow string is a warlock that has formed a pact with a shinigami. When ambushing, it has a corpse on hand, which it puppeteers moments before attacking. If it thinks it will be exposed to harm, it also casts mirror image before the fight. In combat, it tries to keep its distance, lining up foes with which to eviscerate using ice moon, and casting hellish rebuke on any combatants that hit it. If its eldritch blasts are blocked (for example, by cover), or a foe is especially well armored, it switches to pins & needles. After its third puppet has died, it runs away, using misty step if available.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 372 (Indie)

monster.name: Shinobi Adept

trait.name: Nimble Duelist
trait.text: Creatures that the adept hits with its Rapier attack (or any other Finesse weapon) can't make opportunity attacks against it until the end of the turn.

trait.name: Spells
trait.text:
The adept is a 7th-level spellcaster. It uses Intelligence as its spellcasting ability (spell save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks). The adept has the following wizard spells prepared:
• At will: dash strike*, incendiary strike*, smokescreen*(c), soften descent* • 2/Day each: flashbang*, hellish rebuke, invisibility(c), shield • 1/Day each: flaming tiger leap*, fly(c), slatestorm*(c), steelskin*(p)
*See Chapter 13 - Spells; c = concentration; p = pre-cast before combat

trait.name: Additional Spells
trait.text: The adept has two other wizard spells of 1st level or higher prepared from its spellbook (GM's choice). It can cast any of those other spells in place of a spell of equal level from the list above.

action.name: Multiattack
action.text: The adept makes two Rapier or Hand Crossbow attacks.

action.name: Rapier
action.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Hand Crossbow
action.text: Ranged Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) piercing damage.

action.name: Shadow Striker (Bonus Action)
action.text:
When the adept hits with a weapon attack roll on its turn, it can expend one use of a spell to deal an extra 7 (2d6) damage of the attack's type and cause one of the following effects:
Cloud of Shadow. The adept creates a cloud of magical darkness in the space it currently occupies that lasts for 1 minute.
Enervate. The target must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take an extra 3 (1d6) poison damage and be poisoned until the start of the adept's next turn.
Shadow Ward. The adept gains resistance to a damage type other than force or radiant (adept's choice) until the start of its next turn.
Shadow Vanishing. Immediately after the adept makes the attack, it spends 10 feet of movement to teleport to an area of dim light or darkness it can see within 30 feet of it.

monster.spells: Dash Strike (HB), Incendiary Strike (HB), Smokescreen (TP), Soften Descent (HB), Flashbang (HB), Hellish Rebuke, Invisibility, Shield, Flaming Tiger Leap (HB), Fly, Slatestorm (HB), Steelskin (HB)
monster.description:
The shinobi adept is a wizard unafraid to move into the frontlines. It typically starts combat having already cast the steelskin spell and casts slatestorm as its first action, using its movement to cause as many creatures as possible to be affected by the spell. For its second turn, it tries to position itself to hit at least two creatures with flaming tiger leap, using the movement to bring as many people as possible into the range of the slatestorm spell, and landing next to a low AC target. From then on, it uses its weapon attacks and Shadow Striker bonus action to create Clouds of Shadow, which it moves between using Shadow Vanishing; most enemies won't know which shadow to target. When ambushing, it springs its trap with flashbang. To flee, it casts invisibility or smokescreen, moves out of melee range, and then casts fly.
Source: Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realm p. 373 (Indie)
